items.title: Sabah allocates RM102m for food security to tackle cost of living
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government will focus on food security to mitigate the medium- and long-term impacts of the rising cost of living. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that under the 13th Malaysia Plan, RM102.86 million has been allocated for food security projects. Hajiji said the government, through the Sabah Economic Development and Investment Authority (Sedia), has identified Keningau, Tenom, Nabawan, Tongod, and Tambunan as Food Production Valleys.

items.title: DBKL to address traffic congestion at critical locations through road upgrading projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is implementing three targeted road projects at critical locations identified based on an analysis of current traffic flows and projected vehicle growth. He said the project involves the construction of a road and tunnel from Jalan Kiara 7 to the SPRINT Highway (Penchala Link) in Segambut to address congestion on Jalan Desa Kiara and the Mont Kiara area which is at a critical level (LOS F) due to access and exit constraints as well as increased development density. This project involves the construction of a 562-metre new road as well as tunnel and bridge structures.

items.title: Perak maintains zero tolerance on bullying after MRSM incident
items.summary: PERAK: The state government will await a full report before deciding on action over an alleged bullying incident at Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) Sultan Azlan Shah in Kuala Kangsar. State Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah said authorities have yet to receive a comprehensive account of the case. "In my view, the state government and the State Education Department have zero tolerance when it comes to bullying cases.

items.title: World's first AI-powered homes to debut in i-City at competitive pricing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's residential landscape is set for a significant shift as I-Bhd prepares to launch AI Living, the country's first robot-enabled residential development, at i-City this October, with prices starting from RM1,050 per sq ft. Positioned as a core component of a 1.75 hectare mixed-use development with a gross development value (GDV) of RM1.2 billion, AI Living, also the world's first of its kind residential project, will comprise a 46-storey tower housing 500 residential units. Unlike conventional developments where smart features are retrofitted, AI Living has been conceived as an "AI-native" project from inception.

items.title: Scam content takedowns hit 43,618 in Q1, Teo Nie Ching calls trend worrying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — A total of 43,618 scam-related content was removed by social media platforms as of the first quarter of this year, said Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching. She said the removals were carried out at the request of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), with platform providers complying with the applications. Teo said the number of scam-related content taken down has risen sharply each year, with 6,297 cases recorded in 2023, increasing to 63,652 in 2024 and 98,503 in 2025.

items.title: Akmal backs BN pullout, says move rooted in Umno principles
items.summary: AYER KEROH: Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has backed the move by 14 Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen to withdraw their support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, describing it as a principled decision. Dr Akmal, who is also BN Youth chief, said the firm stand taken by Negri Sembilan Umno proves the party will defend its core struggle for religion, race, and the royal institution, even at the risk of losing power. "I congratulate the Negri Sembilan Umno leadership for having the courage to make a decision based on party principles.

items.title: DOE warns of jail, million‑ringgit fines as probe launched into Terengganu peatland fire
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The Department of Environment (DOE) has opened an investigation into parties suspected of carrying out open burning in connection with a forest fire in a peatland area at Kampung Baru, Kuala Abang in Dungun, Terengganu, on April 22. The DOE views the forest fire incident in the peatland area seriously and that an investigation has been initiated under Section 29A of the Environmental Quality Act 1974. Hence, the department, in line with its jurisdiction under the Environmental Quality Act 1974, has also taken several measures, including continuous air quality monitoring through the installation of portable monitoring equipment (GRIMM) in areas near the fire location.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry finalising transfer of Form 6, matriculation programmes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is finalising the legal and administrative framework to bring the Form Six and matriculation programmes under its jurisdiction. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said this followed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's recent announcement on moving pre-university programmes from the Education Ministry to the Higher Education Ministry. He said the transfer of the programmes to the Higher Education Ministry would not disrupt the financial aid.

items.title: KLIA's passenger movements grows 14.4pct to 16.9mil in Q1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded strong growth across its local network in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, with Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) registering 16.9 million passenger movements, a 14.4 per cent increase compared to the same period last year. MAHB said during the same month, Penang International Airport (PEN) saw an 11 per cent rise in international passenger numbers, moving from 371,601 in February to 412,958. MAHB said new routes introduced include Nanjing–Kuala Lumpur, Chongqing–Kota Kinabalu, Wuxi–Kuala Lumpur, Batam–Kuala Lumpur, Phuket–Penang, Guiyang–Kuala Lumpur, Guiyang–Kuching, Colombo–Kuala Lumpur and Yangzhou–Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Bumiputera Education Congress to review key education policies in July
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Bumiputera Education Congress 2026 will be held in July to review key education policies and strengthen the national Bumiputera education agenda. Head Secretariat of the Bumiputera Education Congress 2026 Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi said the one-day congress will bring together more than 4,000 participants from universities, government agencies, NGOs and political parties. He said the congress will feature 25 presenters covering five main themes, including a review of existing policies such as matriculation and fully residential schools.

items.title: Negri Sembilan DAP leaders hold emergency meeting over state crisis
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Several Negri Sembilan DAP assemblymen were seen gathering at the state DAP headquarters in Kemayan Square here today following Barisan Nasional's (BN) withdrawal of support for the state government. The meeting is believed to be an emergency session to discuss the leadership crisis involving Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, who is also Chennah assemblyman, arrived around 2pm to chair the meeting.

items.title: Edotco Malaysia's unit Vista Bumiria leads telecom infra drive in Terengganu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Edotco Malaysia's subsidiary Vista Bumiria Sdn Bhd has been approinted as state-backed company to drive the development and expansion of telecommunications infrastructure in Terengganu. The Terengganu state government also designated Alam Mindscape Mobile Sdn Bhd as the one stop agency for telecommunications towers. "This strategic move reflects Terengganu's commitment to accelerating the rollout of high-quality, future-ready digital infrastructure, while ensuring more coordinated, efficient and sustainable development across the state," Edotco said in a statement.

items.title: Final verdict for Syed Saddiq appeal on June 30
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court has fixed June 30 to deliver its final verdict in the appeal involving Muar member of Parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman. Deputy Public Prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin confirmed the date when contacted. The appeal before the apex court stems from the prosecution's bid to overturn the Court of Appeal's decision which acquitted Syed Saddiq of charges linked to criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering involving Armada funds.

items.title: Pilgrims warned: ‘No Visa, No Haj’ as Putrajaya enforces strict compliance with Saudi rules
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The Tiada Visa, Tiada Haji (No Visa, No Haj) compliance campaign has been launched to safeguard the welfare, safety and well being of pilgrims, curb fraud and ensure compliance with the regulations set by the Saudi government. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Datuk Dr Zulkifli Hasan, said the campaign is being implemented in response to the Saudi government’s call to promote a similar approach. Zulkifli said the campaign also aligns with the Muzakarah Haji’s ruling on the importance of complying with haj visa regulations.

items.title: Uzma Engineering secures Petronas Carigali contract for Bayan Phase 3 PWIM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Uzma Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Uzma Engineering Sdn Bhd, has secured a Portable Water Injection Module (PWIM) contract from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd under the Bayan Redevelopment Phase 3 project, strengthening its production solutions segment. The contract covers the provision of leasing, as well as operation and maintenance services for the PWIM. Uzma group chief executive officer Datuk Kamarul Redzuan Muhamed said the contract reflects the group's continued efforts to strengthen its capabilities across the production solutions space, positioning it to support operators at various stages of field development.

items.title: Shafee tight-lipped after Najib drops appeal in addendum case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah remained tight-lipped following Datuk Seri Najib Razak's decision to withdraw his appeal over the failed bid to serve the remainder of his prison sentence under house arrest. Earlier, it was reported that the former prime minister had withdrawn his appeal against the High Court's dismissal of his bid to serve the remainder of his jail term under house arrest. Law firm Messrs Shafee & Co, which represented Najib, said in a letter to the Court of Appeal that the former prime minister intended to withdraw the appeal without leave to refile and without any order as to costs.

items.title: Chinese citizen held for driving against traffic in Sungai Petani
items.summary: The incident went viral on social media today after the man's dangerous driving was captured on camera. Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police received a report on the two-car collision at 11.30pm. The driver of the other car escaped uninjured, Hanyan said.

items.title: Police probe fatal Klang crash after viral video shows bystanders breaking mirror, moving vehicle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Police are investigating a fatal accident in Pandamaran, Klang, after a viral video showed several individuals breaking a vehicle’s mirror and attempting to move it following the crash. In a statement, South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident occurred on April 26 at about 8.30pm along Jalan Bukit Kerayong. Preliminary investigations found the crash involved a Toyota Avanza driven by a local man in his 60s, who was travelling from Jalan Bukit Kerayong towards Jalan Banting Pandamaran.

items.title: Gold Li eyes RM15mil from IPO, sets price at 13 sen a share
items.summary: The IPO will entail 153 million shares, comprising 117 million new shares and 36 million existing shares for Bumiputera investors approved by Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry. Of the 117 million new shares, 30 million will be available for the Malaysian public, six million for the property company's eligible persons, 39 million for Bumiputera investors approved by the ministry, and the remaining 42 million for the private placement to selected investors. Upon full subscription, the Johor-focused property developer's share capital would be valued at RM122.96 million placing its total market capitalisation at RM78 million.

items.title: Hari Raya travel surge lifts KLIA traffic, MAHB reports 14.4pc rise in Q1 passenger movements
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded strong growth across its local network in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, with Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) registering 16.9 million passenger movements, a 14.4 per cent increase compared to the same period last year. MAHB said during the same month, Penang International Airport (PEN) saw an 11 per cent rise in international passenger numbers, moving from 371,601 in February to 412,958. MAHB said new routes introduced include Nanjing-Kuala Lumpur, Chongqing-Kota Kinabalu, Wuxi-Kuala Lumpur, Batam-Kuala Lumpur, Phuket-Penang, Guiyang-Kuala Lumpur, Guiyang-Kuching, Colombo-Kuala Lumpur and Yangzhou-Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Media Council resolves five public complaints since establishment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has received seven public complaints since its establishment in June last year, ranging from reporting accuracy to the unauthorised use of images. Teo said the council, as an independent self-regulatory body, aims to reinforce ethical standards and safeguard media freedom. She said the MMC should continue to serve as the main platform and first point of reference for handling public complaints involving the media.

items.title: No agility, no resilience: A wake-up call for Malaysia's manufacturers
items.summary: MANUFACTURING sits at the heart of Malaysia's industrial ambitions and export competitiveness. Yet, amid wild fluctuations in global trade, the nation's manufacturers face mounting pressure from macroeconomic headwinds, supply chain disruptions, rising input costs and increasingly complex operational demands. Simultaneously, a fealty to traditional production models means Malaysian manufacturers risk being left behind amid an increasingly volatile environment marked by rapid demand shifts, increased digital adoption, and supply chain disruptions.

items.title: Police probe Facebook user for insulting Sultan of Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have launched an investigation into a Facebook account owner over a post deemed insulting to the Sultan of Selangor. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the April 25 post by the Facebook account 'Huayan The Pureland Learning Centre' has been traced, with action underway. "Investigations against the individual are ongoing, including the seizure of devices used to upload the post by the CID's Classified Criminal Investigation Unit, Prosecution and Legal Division (D5), in collaboration with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).

items.title: Politicising FWCMS may set back progress made in managing foreign workers: Mamba
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Politicising Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) could undermine the progress made in improving governance, efficiency and transparency in foreign worker recruitment, Malaysian Micro Business Association (Mamba) said. Mamba president Tan Peng Beng said the implementation of FWCMS and wider digitalisation efforts had helped resolve longstanding weaknesses in the foreign worker recruitment system. Tan said micro businesses and SMEs including hawkers, market traders, pasar malam operators, small sundry shop owners and workshops are the backbone of Malaysia's economy and depend heavily on migrant workers to sustain daily operations.

items.title: Johor Pas signals early push to unseat Syed Saddiq in Muar
items.summary: MUAR: Pas has signalled an early push to capture the Muar parliamentary seat from incumbent Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman. Johor Pas commissioner Datuk Dr Mahfodz Mohamed confirmed the party is preparing for a tougher contest at the next general election. Mahfodz said the party has begun strengthening its election machinery in the constituency and across the district, anticipating an intense multi-cornered fight.

items.title: RM26mil allocated for Menu Kasih Siswa programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry has introduced the Menu Kasih Siswa programme, with an allocation of RM26 million. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said university administrators should not wait for formal ministerial launches before rolling out the initiative. "Regarding private higher education institutions, we are taking two approaches.

items.title: Sabah rolls out support measures to keep tourism resilient as fuel costs bite
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 27 — Sabah is planning targeted support measures, incentives and stronger strategic marketing to sustain its tourism supply chain and ease pressure on industry players amid rising fuel costs. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the move aims to maintain demand and air connectivity to Sabah during this challenging period. Jafry said global conflicts and rising air travel costs could directly affect Sabah’s tourism sector, which relies heavily on international arrivals and air connectivity.

items.title: Negri Sembilan MB to meet Ruler as BN withdraws support
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun is believed to have sought an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today following a state leadership crisis. Earlier today, state BN chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias held a press conference to announce the withdrawal of support, citing a crisis involving state constitutional and customary institutions. The political turmoil follows a controversial move by the Undang Luak (territorial chiefs), who reportedly declared the removal of Tuanku Muhriz at the Balai Undang Luak Sungai Ujong.

items.title: [UPDATED] PN offers to form new Negri Sembilan govt with BN
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Perikatan Nasional (PN) has offered to collaborate with Barisan Nasional (BN) to form a new state government, hours after all 14 BN assemblymen withdrew their support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. State opposition leader and Labu assemblyman Mohamad Hanifah Abu Baker announced the decision following an emergency meeting held here today. Hanifah said the five PN assemblymen are ready to work with the 14 BN representatives to ensure administrative stability and the continued development of the state.

items.title: Malaysia shifts focus to tech fields in Türkiye education collaboration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is shifting its higher education collaboration with Türkiye towards technology and technical fields, moving beyond traditional areas such as literature and history. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the move will be a key focus of the second Malaysia–Türkiye Joint Committee (JC) meeting, which Malaysia will host this year. Zambry said Malaysia has identified suitable higher education institutions in Türkiye for collaboration, particularly in areas such as artificial intelligence (AI), aerospace, green technology, sustainability and digital transformation.

items.title: Ex‑telecoms director pleads not guilty to false statement charge dating back 14 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — A former telecommunications company director pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of submitting a document containing false statements related to independent consulting engineer (ICE) services 14 years ago. Deputy public prosecutor Vivien Yeap Jie Xi proposed bail at RM20,000 with two Malaysian sureties, along with additional conditions for the accused to surrender his passport and report to the MACC office monthly. Judge Mohd Kafli Che Ali granted bail at RM20,000 with two Malaysian sureties.

items.title: Melor assemblyman to give statement over viral video mocking police
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud will present himself at the Kota Baru district police headquarters this afternoon to provide a statement regarding allegations of disparaging the police. Wan Rohimi is expected to hold a press conference following the process at 5pm. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the probe is being conducted under Section 504 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955.

items.title: EU steelmakers set for rebound as Iran war hurts more exposed Asian peers
items.summary: After over half a dozen muted earnings seasons, steelmakers in the European Union (EU) are set for a rebound and the first quarter of 2026 could be an inflection point, analysts say. Though demand has not recovered to 2022 levels, steel prices have increased faster than expected in recent months thanks to higher energy prices and diminishing imports from outside the bloc due to the EU's new safeguards. European hot rolled coil prices have gained about 20 per cent in the past six months.

items.title: Welfare Dept seeks families of two abandoned babies in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Welfare Department is seeking to trace the biological parents of a baby girl found in a rubbish disposal area at a flats complex in Taman Plentong Utama here on March 9. District welfare officer Khairu Asri Ismail said, based on a police report, a resident at Block G heard a baby crying at 8.40am and traced the sound. The infant, with her umbilical cord still attached, was found abandoned at the flats' rubbish disposal area.

items.title: China vows countermeasures if EU enacts 'Made in Europe' plan
items.summary: BEIJING: Beijing slammed on Monday an EU plan aimed to bolster the bloc's industries against fierce competition from China, vowing countermeasures if it is enacted. The EU unveiled in March new "Made in Europe" rules for companies trying to access public funds in strategic sectors including cars, green tech and steel, obliging firms to meet minimum thresholds for EU-made parts. Beijing's commerce ministry said on Monday that it had submitted comments to the European Commission on Friday, expressing China's "serious concerns" regarding the act it called "systemic discrimination."

items.title: Oil price hike does not guarantee higher Sabah revenue
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The rise in global oil prices does not necessarily translate into higher state revenue for oil-producing Sabah. State Assistant Finance Minister Datuk Mohd Ishak Ayub said the conflict in the Middle East since early March 2026 has led to a significant increase in global oil prices. "However, the increase in prices does not fully translate into higher state revenue.

items.title: Police probe Facebook post allegedly insulting Selangor Sultan, say account holder identified
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Police have launched an investigation into a social media post allegedly containing insulting imagery of the Sultan of Selangor, according to an official statement today. In a statement, Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the post was uploaded on April 25 via a Facebook account identified as “Huayan The Pureland Learning Centre”. He said the case is being investigated under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, Section 504 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.

items.title: Deputy minister hopes Media Council becomes first stop for press complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Deputy Communication Minister Teo Nie Ching today expressed hope that the police would accord the newly set-up Media Council more trust to deal with press-related complaints, even if it still has authority to investigate them under criminal laws. “But I still hope when the Malaysian Media Council’s role become stronger it can become the first layer, or the first platform to deal with affairs relating to the media,” the Kulai MP (DAP) added. The MMC was formally established following the gazettement of the Malaysian Media Council Act 2024 on June 13, 2025.

items.title: BN to consult chairman on N. Sembilan govt after reps withdraw support for MB, says sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said he would refer to the top BN leadership following the action of 14 Negeri Sembilan Umno/BN assemblymen withdrawing support for the Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, before issuing an official statement. He said he also needed to obtain detailed information and discuss with the top leadership before issuing any official statement on the status of the state government. “I will refer this matter to the Barisan Nasional chairman (Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi) first,” he told reporters after the Higher Education Minister’s media briefing for the Second Joint Committee (JC) Meeting between Malaysia and Turkey and the Bumiputera Education Congress at Menara Dato Onn here today.

items.title: RM30mil Media Innovation Fund to boost local media industry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry has allocated RM30 million under the Media Innovation Fund to strengthen the local media industry through grants of up to RM300,000 per application. She said Phase Five of the Media Innovation Fund will open from Friday (May 1) until May 14. "The Media Innovation Fund has progressed through four phases to date, with Phase One completed in January 2026, involving 34 organisations receiving RM12.38 million for digital transformation.

items.title: Five N. Sembilan Perikatan reps say ‘ready’ to work with 14 BN lawmakers, stop short of backing no-confidence move
items.summary: Negeri Sembilan’s opposition leader Mohamed Hanifer Abu Backer, who is also Labu state assemblymen, represented all the five PN state lawmakers in saying so. “In relation with that, the five Negeri Sembilan state assemblymen from Perikatan Nasional are “bersedia” (prepared) to work together with 14 Negeri Sembilan Umno BN state assemblymen to ensure the stability of the state government and continuity of the state government’s administration to develop Negeri Sembilan and ensure the wellbeing of Negeri Sembilan,” he said in a press conference at the Royale Chulan Seremban here. The Negeri Sembilan state assembly has 36 state assemblymen, with 17 from Pakatan Harapan, 14 from Umno and five from PN.

items.title: Selangor targets hydrogen-powered Smart buses by year-end, says exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 27 — The Selangor government is targeting the use of hydrogen energy for the Smart Selangor Bus service by the end of this year, as part of efforts to promote more sustainable energy use. He added that there are currently 1,090 electric vehicle (EV) charging stations operating in Selangor, with the highest number in Shah Alam (226), followed by Kajang (221), Sepang (182) and Subang Jaya (145). Other areas include Petaling Jaya (68), Selayang (53), Ampang Jaya (49), Kuala Langat (47), Kuala Selangor (43), Klang (43), Hulu Selangor (12) and one unit in Sabak Bernam.

items.title: MyJPJ app gets fresh redesign with new interface update
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has rolled out an update to its MyJPJ mobile application on Android and iOS, introducing a refreshed user interface for users nationwide. The update also introduces a shortcut to the inbox, allowing users to view notifications linked to their accounts, along with directory access and profile management features. Users can update or download the latest version of the MyJPJ app via the Google Play Store and Apple App Store to access the new interface.

items.title: Farmer charged with murder over Kota Tinggi shooting that killed three at restaurant
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 27 — A 71-year-old farmer was charged at a Magistrates’ Court here for the fatal shooting involving two men and a woman at a restaurant last week. No plea was recorded as murder cases fall under the High Court’s jurisdiction. According to the charge sheet, Lim is accused of shooting dead two men, identified as Wong Poh Soon, 61, and Chen Kwai Hwa, 64, together with a Vietnamese woman, Tran Thi Trang, 37, at a restaurant at Jalan Delima in Taman Kota Jaya here at about 1.30pm on April 19.

items.title: Defence Ministry ramps up reskilling, aims half of ex‑servicemen secure jobs in private sector
items.summary: Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said Mindef, through the Armed Forces Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat), is now placing greater emphasis on industry-based transition training, with personnel required to undergo programmes aligned with current labour market needs. Among the initiatives implemented by Mindef are the Prowira Readiness-to-Work programme, the Veteran Reskilling and Upskilling Programme, and the JanaVeteran Programme. Mohamed Khaled said that the government was also funding diploma programmes for serving members in preparation for their transition after completing their service.

items.title: Sabah faces rising sinkhole cases as ageing sewerage lines collapse after decades
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 27 — Sabah’s sewerage pipes – many going back 20 to 30 years – are crumbling and causing sinkholes (pic), burst pipes and leaks, with most parts of the State still depending on septic tanks instead of proper treatment systems. “The pipes were constructed years ago and we are now seeing a lot of complaints coming in from localised hotspots, leakages and bursting pipes,” said State Sewerage Service Department Chief Assistant Director Jenni Alliviana Suallih. Jenni said physical development was outpacing the sewerage systems meant to support it.

items.title: BN mum on Melaka election strategy, says decision still ‘party secret’
items.summary: According to a report by Utusan Malaysia , Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter remains an internal party decision and would not be disclosed at this stage. On another matter, Mohamed Khaled said the “Rumah Bangsa” initiative is not limited to bringing former Umno members back into the party but also serves as an open platform to attract youth participation. He said the initiative is designed to make it easier for young people to get involved without being burdened by procedural barriers that may discourage entry.

items.title: Seven‑hour ordeal in darkness as man threatens wife and kids, Tawau police storm in to save victims
items.summary: Tawau District Police Chief, ACP Jasmin Hussin said police received information about the incident at around 8.30pm after the suspect acted aggressively at his home. A team from the Criminal Investigation Division (CID) IPD Tawau led by the Head of District Criminal Investigation DSP Zulham Affendi Zulkifli together with officers from the Kalabakan Police Station were deployed to the scene. “Police took about seven hours to persuade the aggressive suspect before storming in to rescue the victims.

items.title: Penang launches RM20m Climate Mitigation Fund to help SMEs adopt greener operations
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 27 — Penang launched a new Climate Mitigation Fund (CM Fund) with an initial RM20 million to help manufacturers implement sustainable solutions and reduce carbon emissions. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the fund is a collaboration between Alliance Bank Malaysia Berhad and the Penang state government under the Penang Green Industry Programme (PGIP). He said Alliance has also committed RM100 million to the fund.

items.title: Penang’s single-use plastic ban on track for Sept 1, shift to biodegradable alternatives
items.summary: Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state government is already looking into alternative materials that resemble plastic but are biodegradable. He said the proposed replacement materials must possess characteristics similar to conventional plastic to ensure they remain practical for everyday use. “We will look for alternatives that look like plastic, feel like plastic and are used like plastic, but are actually not plastic because they are made from biodegradable materials,” he said.

items.title: DBKL launches Projek Trafik Lancar, major road upgrades to ease congestion in Segambut, Seputeh and Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is rolling three targeted roadwork projects to ease congestion across the capital. Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri TPr Fadlun Mak Ujud said the initiative, known as Projek Trafik Lancar (Smooth Traffic Project), marks a shift in how the city manages development funds, ensuring that revenue collected from new projects is reinvested directly into surrounding communities, with the project targeting three Parliament constituencies — Segambut, Seputeh and Wangsa Maju. “This project will utilise ‘building charges’ or development charges that were collected from ongoing development projects in these areas to serve this purpose — upgrading of the surroundings of development projects.

items.title: LDP president says Sabah deserves more funds, urges Putrajaya to honour MA63 commitments
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 27 — The federal government must have stronger commitment towards implementing the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) and increasing development allocations for Sabah, said Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) president Datuk Chin Su Phin. “Even as legal proceedings continue, the federal government’s responsibilities to Sabah remain and must not be used as justification to delay or weaken the implementation of Sabah’s rights,” he said during the party’s 6th Supreme Council Meeting yesterday. On fiscal matters, Chin noted that Sabah appreciated the federal government’s continued provision of special allocations, including about RM600 million in 2025, but voiced concerns that the allocation for 2026 may remain unchanged.

items.title: Man identified in Kedah punching incident after viral Titi Gajah video
items.summary: According to a report by Harian Metro , Kota Setar district police chief ACP Syed Basri Syed Ali confirmed that police had received a report over the incident, which is believed to have taken place on April 20 at about 10am in front of the victim’s house in Titi Gajah. The 38-year-old victim was said to have had a dispute with the suspect’s wife two days before the incident. Medical staff later advised the victim to lodge a police report for further action.

items.title: Kuching police arrest man, 32, over alleged molest of two minors at apartment
items.summary: KUCHING, April 27 — Police have arrested a 32-year-old man for allegedly molesting two minors at an apartment here last Saturday. “Acting on information, IPD Kuching Criminal Investigation Department (CID) successfully arrested a 32-year-old local male suspect,” it said. IPD Kuching said initial urine screening showed the suspect was negative for drugs, and further checks revealed no prior criminal record.

items.title: No boundary change: Sarawak assemblyman insists Singai remains part of Bau under Greater Bau initiative
items.summary: KUCHING, April 27 — Tasik Biru assemblyman Datuk Henry Harry Jinep has dismissed claims that several villages in Singai area would be transferred to Petra Jaya under the Greater Bau initiative. The Deputy Transport Minister stressed that Singai will remain under the administration of the Bau district. He noted that since Datuk Peter Nansian Ngusie became an elected representative, and later himself, the size and boundaries of Bau District have remain unchanged.

items.title: N. Sembilan Umno bloc withdraws support for MB Aminuddin Harun, claims loss of confidence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said all 14 Umno/Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan have lost confidence in Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and withdrawn their support for the state government under his leadership. Jalaluddin said the bloc had reached a unanimous decision, signalling a significant shift in support within the state assembly. “We also wish to emphasise that we fully support and uphold the adat institutions, the Constitution, and the Laws of the Constitution of the Negeri Sembilan state government.

items.title: TTDI park upgrade on track for June reopening despite nearby wall collapse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Upgrading works at Taman Persekutuan Lembah Kiara in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) are expected to be completed by June, with authorities saying a recent retaining wall collapse nearby will not affect the project timeline. According to a report by The Star , National Landscape Department (JLN) director-general Mansor Mohamad said the park upgrading project is now 98 per cent complete and remains on schedule, pending handover from the contractor. Mansor said the earliest possible reopening of the park is in June, depending on when the contractor completes handover to JLN.

items.title: Deputy minister says Haj operations smooth, urges pilgrims to stay healthy before departure
items.summary: SINTOK, April 27 — Haj arrangements for Malaysian pilgrims have so far proceeded smoothly, with flight operations to the Holy Land now in the active phase involving about 100 flights in total, Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli said. On the J-KIS programme, she said it aimed to raise awareness of social issues and mental health among university students, while equipping them with skills to manage emotions, conflicts and life challenges. “The programme featured various slots reflecting the realities faced by students today, including mental health issues, risky behaviour, substance abuse, identity formation, digital literacy and social media ethics, with almost 600 students from various institutions taking part,” she said.

items.title: Malaysia’s strength in Islamic finance hailed as model for Muslim nations to resist Western networks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — Turkiye’s Necmeddin Bilal Erdogan has called on Muslim nations to learn from Malaysia’s Islamic finance sector to build financial systems that are not dependent on Western networks. Speaking as one of the panellists at the 11th Forum Ilmuwan Malaysia Madani (FIM), he said Malaysia’s strength in Islamic finance offers a practical blueprint for collaboration. “Malaysia is strong in Islamic finance, which other Muslim countries can take lessons from and do collaborative efforts in building those financial systems so that we are not dependent on Western financial networks,” he said.

items.title: MACC freezes fertiliser maker Cropmate’s bank accounts in money laundering probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Bank accounts belonging to fertiliser manufacturer Cropmate Bhd and its subsidiaries have been frozen as part of an ongoing money laundering investigation by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). According to a report by The Edge , the company confirmed it was notified on April 24 that several of its accounts had been frozen under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA). “The board does not expect this matter to have any material financial or operational impact, and the company’s day-to-day operations remain fully functional,” it said.
